name: track_package_current_status
description: Get current location and status of a package. Can lookup by order ID (will find tracking number from WMS) or directly by tracking number. Use this when customer asks 'where is my package' or 'what's the status of my order'.

order_id.description: Order identifier like ORD-001. Use this if customer provides order number.

tracking_number.description: Carrier tracking number. Use this if customer provides tracking number directly.

name: get_shipment_history
description: Get complete tracking timeline for a shipment including all scan events, delivery proof, and signature info. Use when customer asks for detailed tracking history or delivery confirmation.

name: check_order_fulfillment_status
description: Check if an order has been picked, packed, and handed over to carrier. Use when customer asks 'has my order shipped yet' or wants to know warehouse processing status.

name: check_inventory_levels
description: Check current stock levels for a product SKU. Use before promising reshipments or when customer asks about product availability.

sku.description: Product SKU to check inventory for

warehouse_region.description: Optional specific warehouse region to check

name: check_customs_status
description: Check customs clearance status for international shipments. Use when customer asks about delays on international orders or when tracking shows customs processing.

tracking_number.description: The carrier tracking number for the international shipment

name: expedite_customs_clearance
description: Request priority customs processing for VIP customers or SLA breaches. This is a write operation that creates an expedite request.

priority_reason.description: Business justification for expediting (e.g., 'VIP customer', 'SLA breach')

name: reroute_package
description: Request to change delivery address for a package that hasn't been delivered yet. This is a write operation that attempts to reroute the shipment.

name: schedule_redelivery
description: Schedule a new delivery attempt after a failed delivery. This is a write operation that books a delivery slot.

preferred_date.description: Preferred delivery date in YYYY-MM-DD format
